product_name: Student Support Triage and Signposting Tool

home.title: A calmer starting point when things feel hard to untangle
home.intro: This tool is designed to help you work out a sensible first step. You only need to answer a few short questions.
home.student_id_help: Enter your student ID so we can show the course and assessment information linked to your account.
home.category_text: What feels closest to the kind of help you need right now?

categories.label: Studies, deadlines, applications, or next steps
categories.description: Coursework, exams, academic processes, job or postgraduate applications, or pressure around what to focus on next.

categories.label: How you have been feeling day to day
categories.description: Stress, low mood, loneliness, homesickness, or feeling like things are getting too much.

academic_concern.title: What feels closest to what is going on?
academic_concern.text: Choose the option that sounds most like the main kind of help you need.

options.label: I am trying to stop study, deadline, or application pressure from turning into a missed deadline or a bigger problem.
Something has already gone wrong with university work, an assessment, a deadline, or a related academic process.
I mainly need help with understanding, planning, or improving my work.
I am feeling stuck about careers, applications, or what I should focus on next.

assessment_choice_prevent.title: Which assessment or assessments are being affected?
assessment_choice_prevent.text: Choose every piece of university work that feels part of this situation right now.

assessment_choice_setback.title: Which assessment or assessments are affected by what has happened?
assessment_choice_setback.text: Choose every piece of university work affected by this so far.

assessment_choice_skill.title: Which assessment or assessments is this mainly about?
assessment_choice_skill.text: Choose the work you want this support to be tied to.

academic_prevent_step.title: What would help most right now?
academic_prevent_step.text: Pick the kind of help that would make the next step clearer.

options.label: I need to understand whether I can ask for more time.
I need clear guidance on what I should do before the deadline gets closer.
I need practical study or revision support so I can get back on track.

academic_setback.title: What best describes what has happened?
academic_setback.text: Choose the closest fit so we can point you to the most relevant next step.

options.label: I missed a submission or I know I will miss it.
I missed an exam or the exam was badly affected by serious circumstances.
I already have a decision or result and I think there may be grounds to question it.

academic_skill_area.title: What kind of academic support feels most useful?
academic_skill_area.text: Choose the area that would help you feel steadier with the selected work.

options.label: Writing, structure, or planning my coursework.
Maths, data, or statistics support.
Academic English or confidence using academic language.
Revision, exam preparation, or understanding what is expected.

academic_career_type.title: What feels closest on the career side?
academic_career_type.text: Choose the option that sounds most like the support you need first.

options.label: Application deadlines or application preparation are taking over.
I am unsure what to do next for jobs, placements, postgraduate study, or PhD plans.
Career planning or applications are starting to affect my university work as well.

academic_career_study_overlap.title: Is university work being affected as well?
academic_career_study_overlap.text: Choose the option that fits best right now.

options.label: Yes — one or more university assessments or deadlines are being affected too.
No — this is mainly about careers, applications, or next steps right now.

assessment_choice_career.title: Which assessment or assessments are being affected as well?
assessment_choice_career.text: Choose every piece of university work that has been knocked by this as well.

academic_route_ai1.title: A practical place to start before this gets harder to manage
academic_route_ai1.summary: The most useful first step here is to deal with the time-sensitive part clearly, then add any study support you still need.

academic_route_ai2.title: A calm place to start after an assessment problem
academic_route_ai2.summary: The most helpful next move is to look at the guidance that fits what has already happened, one step at a time.

academic_route_ai3.title: Support that can help you feel steadier with the selected work
academic_route_ai3.summary: It looks like practical academic support is the best first step here, rather than a formal process.

academic_route_ai4.title: A clearer starting point for career planning and application pressure
academic_route_ai4.summary: It looks like you need a focused career-support route that helps you act on the right next step without letting applications and university work blur into one pile.

wellbeing_urgency.title: How does this feel right now?
wellbeing_urgency.text: Choose the closest fit. The wording does not have to be perfect.

options.label: It feels urgent and I may need help straight away.
It is making day-to-day life or study hard to manage.
It is not urgent, but I know I need some support.

wellbeing_theme.title: What feels closest to the main issue?
wellbeing_theme.text: Pick the option that feels most like what is weighing on you.

options.label: Stress, pressure, or burnout.
Low mood or feeling emotionally flat.
Loneliness, homesickness, or feeling disconnected.

wellbeing_support_style.title: What sounds most manageable as a first step?
wellbeing_support_style.text: Choose the option that feels easiest to act on first.

options.label: Talking to someone directly.
Starting with something quieter that I can look at by myself.
Something that helps me feel less alone.

wellbeing_route_ws1.title: A gentle starting point that does not ask too much of you
wellbeing_route_ws1.summary: It looks like a lower-pressure option may be the best way in, especially if you need something manageable before doing anything more direct.

wellbeing_route_ws2.title: Direct support is likely to help more than handling this alone
wellbeing_route_ws2.summary: It looks like speaking to someone or using a more direct wellbeing route would be the strongest first step.

wellbeing_route_ws3.title: Please use urgent support now
wellbeing_route_ws3.summary: What you described sounds serious enough that urgent support should come first.
